findings.title: — No hardcoded secrets or credentials
findings.description: The API key is hardcoded in the source code.

findings.title: — No hardcoded secrets or credentials
findings.description: The JWT signing secret is hardcoded and should be loaded from an environment variable.

findings.title: Use directional channels when possible
findings.description: The function `Producer` uses a bidirectional channel, which allows both sending and receiving. This can lead to misuse at compile time.

findings.title: SEVERITY — No hardcoded secrets or credentials
findings.description: The function `WriteConfig` hardcodes the error handling for JSON marshalling and file writing, which can lead to security vulnerabilities if an error occurs.

findings.title: Use structured logging only
findings.description: The function uses `fmt.Println` for logging, which is not allowed. Structured logging should be used instead.

findings.title: No fmt.Println for logging
findings.description: The function uses `fmt.Printf` for logging, which does not provide timestamps, levels, or structured fields. Use a structured logger instead.

findings.title: Pre-allocate slices when size is known
findings.description: The slice `names` is not pre-allocated, which can lead to repeated allocations and copying during growth.

findings.title: — Use table-driven tests
findings.description: Tests with multiple cases should use the table-driven pattern with subtests. This makes tests readable, easy to extend, and provides clear failure messages.

findings.title: Exported functions must have doc comments
findings.description: The function `ProcessOrder` is exported but has no doc comment.

findings.title: Use MixedCaps for Go names, not underscores
findings.description: Variable and function names should use MixedCaps (camelCase/PascalCase) instead of underscores.
